@graph.abstract: Which group trip planning app actually gets your friends moving? We compare TRYPS, Wanderlog, Splitwise, Troupe, and SquadTrip side-by-side for 2026 — features, price, and real pros/cons.
keywords: best travel app for group trips

about.name: Best Group Trip Planning App for Friends — 2026 Comparison
No sign-up required for group members — just share a link
Date polling, itinerary building, and expense splitting in one place (not three separate tools)
Free to start — no credit card for the core planning flow
Designed for small friend groups (4–10 people), not enterprise travel teams

mainEntity.name: How does TRYPS differ from Wanderlog for group trips with friends?

acceptedAnswer.text: TRYPS focuses on group coordination first, getting everyone to vote on dates, agree on a shared plan, and settle expenses all in one place, with no sign-up for invitees. Wanderlog is best for building detailed itineraries and mapping, but each group member needs an account, and there is no built-in polling or expense tool.[wanderlog-site]

mainEntity.name: Does Splitwise help plan trips or only split expenses?

acceptedAnswer.text: Splitwise only splits and tracks expenses among friends, with settlement reminders. There is no feature for trip planning, date polling, or itinerary building.[splitwise-site]

mainEntity.name: Which group trip app is best for 4–8 friends planning a weekend away?

acceptedAnswer.text: TRYPS is designed for small friend groups exactly like this, birthdays, reunions, bachelor trips, with built-in date polls, trip planning, and split expense tracking. No separate tools required.[google-travel-trends]

mainEntity.name: Can I use Troupe for expense splitting?

acceptedAnswer.text: Troupe helps groups poll dates and vote on activities or hotels but does not have any built-in expense-tracking tools. You’ll need to use Splitwise or another finance app alongside it.[troupe-site]

mainEntity.name: Is SquadTrip meant for friend group trips?

acceptedAnswer.text: SquadTrip focuses on professional trip hosts or travel businesses who coordinate and collect payments for large, often paid group trips, not casual friend getaways.[squadtrip-site]

mainEntity.name: Do any of these apps require my friends to sign up or download an app?

acceptedAnswer.text: All except TRYPS require friends to either sign up or download an app to join your trip. TRYPS uses a simple invite link or iMessage, no registration required.[google-travel-trends]
TRYPS’s main trip planning flow, date poll, itinerary build, and expense splits, is free to start. There is no credit card required for the core planning functions.[google-travel-trends]

@graph.headline: Wanderlog — Trip planning, itinerary, and travel app
@graph.text: Wanderlog markets itself as a trip planning + itinerary tool with route mapping and offline access; used widely by solo and small-group travelers.

@graph.headline: Splitwise — Split expenses with friends
@graph.text: Splitwise specializes in expense splitting and settlement reminders; does not offer itinerary building or date polling.

@graph.headline: Troupe — Planning group trips together
@graph.text: Troupe focuses on group trip polling and voting on hotels and activities; no built-in expense splitting.

@graph.headline: SquadTrip — Host and manage group trips
@graph.text: SquadTrip is aimed at paid group-trip hosts and travel organizers running multi-trip businesses; different audience from friend-group planners.

@graph.headline: Google — Travel and Tourism Insights
@graph.text: Google's trend data on travel planning behavior, including the rise of multi-friend coordination searches year over year.
